review_note: Medium is the lead; small/large line items are in budget-data-scenarios.json. PR premiums and the $/t-invested and $/t-processed gap with industry bands are flagged in sanity_check.

pricing_assumptions.lme_aluminum_source: LME Aluminium UBC Scrap US (Argus) contract; Westmetall H2 2024-H1 2025 range. https://www.lme.com/metals/non-ferrous/lme-aluminium-ubc-scrap-us-argus
pricing_assumptions.ubc_discount_source: Abralatas 2024 (Brazil UBC 5-15% below LME cash); mid-point for PR contamination premium
pricing_assumptions.lme_hedge_instrument: LME Aluminium 3-mo + Argus UBC basis (LME contract AAAC)
pricing_assumptions.carbon_credit_source: Verra Registry Q1 2025; VCS VM0017/VM0039. https://verra.org

pr_premiums.electricity_premium_source: EIA 2024 ($0.0857/kWh) vs LUMA PREPA filed industrial tariff 2024 ($0.18-0.23/kWh)
pr_premiums.hurricane_construction_premium_source: IBHS FORTIFIED Commercial; R.S. Means 2024; engineering brief §3
pr_premiums.insurance_source: Engineering brief §6; AON PR market update 2024
pr_premiums.construction_labor_source: BLS OEWS PR NAICS 31-33 2024 scarce trades. https://www.bls.gov/oes/current/oes_pr.htm

job_multiplier.multiplier_source: IMPLAN 2023 PR state model NAICS 562 series; cross-checked against BLS RIMS II (range 1.7-3.2x for island-economy recycling)

lines.math: 2 sort lines + ECS + XRF + certified weighbridge; van Dyk / MSS 2024: 4-bay MRF receiving + 2 sort lines $1.8-2.8M equipment; EPC +20% lifts to $2.2-3.4M. vandroek.com
2x 5 t/h industrial shredders + dedensify; UNTHA / Lindner 2024: $700k-$1.1M per line; dual line with redundancy $1.5-2.5M. untha.com
Larger DAF + clarifier train; Hydro International Wash-Box product literature
3 t/h hot-air rotary de-coater, 480-520 C; Hertwich / StrikoWestofen: $3.2-4.6M equipment, install $3.5-5.0M. hertwich.com/products/melting-furnaces

Hertwich URTF-14 tilting rotary, gas-fired; URTF range 3-20 m^3 (6-40 t charge), 1.5-8 t/h melt rate; URTF-14 size class for 15,000 t/yr single-line $6.5-9.0M equipment-and-install. hertwich.com/products/melting-furnaces/universal-rotary-tilting-furnaces
Cooling conveyor + mechanical dross press; Hertwich / Drosstec product literature. Salt-slag recovery optional at +$1.5M (excluded).
Semi-automated sow casting, 5-8 t/h; Wagstaff / Pyrotek: $2.0-2.8M equipment; full system with cooling/stacking/strapping $2.5-3.5M. P1020A-grade casting requires +$1.5M (excluded by design).

Baghouse + RTO + scrubber + dry sorbent injection; JCA Title V-equivalent; EPA Region 2 air permit cost data; Durr / Eisenmann APC budget quotes in Recycling Today 2023-2024. APC train for single rotary furnace of this size $2.5-4.0M.
Above-ground truck scales $200k x 2; on-site XRF lab $400k; forklifts/telehandlers $400k; conveyors and bagging $500-800k
40% x ~$27M equipment; secondary-aluminum EPC industry multiplier 30-45% of equipment cost
3,500 m^2 x ~$2,200/m^2 baseline R.S. Means 2024 + 25% FORTIFIED Gold hurricane premium = $9.6M; sitework/paving/fencing +$1.0M; netting $8.6M. rsmeans.com
PRIDCO-zoned parcel in Caguas-Juncos or Ponce-Penuelas corridor; ~$4.50-5.50/ft^2; PRIDCO 2024 listings; land 3.5 ha = 378,000 ft^2
8-10% of CAPEX standard; OGPe permitting + JCA Title V + DIA $400-600k; commissioning $1.5M; feedstock WC $2.0M; pre-revenue training/G&A $0.5M

medium.source: Inherited from prior baseline; line-by-line sources cross-checked against Hertwich product literature 2024 and R.S. Means 2024.

small.source: small scenario $=5000/t × 5,000 t/yr (medium detail-only); see funding-model PART 1

large.source: large scenario $=2580/t × 40,000 t/yr (medium detail-only); see funding-model PART 1

lines.math: ~2,300 MWh/yr x $0.18/kWh (PR) + 66,000 GJ x $9/GJ. Energy ~26% of OPEX, driven by PR electricity premium. Engineering brief notes Hertwich Ecomelt-PS gas-fired; electric induction alternative adds +$1.5-2.0M/yr energy cost but -80% direct CO2e. eia.gov/electricity/monthly/
62 FTE x $48k loaded = $32k base + ~50% benefits/PR OSHA burden; BLS OEWS PR NAICS 17-2041 (industrial machinery mechanics) 50th percentile 2024 ~$33.4k/hr base. bls.gov/oes/current/oes_pr.htm
Salt flux 30 kg/t x 15,000 t x $0.30/kg = $135k; refractory reline $400k amortized 5 yr = $80k/yr; alloys & minor consumables $615k. Industry rule 5-7% of OPEX for consumables in UBC re-melt.
3.5% x $52M; industry rule secondary aluminum 3-4% of CAPEX/yr
Inbound collection ~600,000 t-km x $0.85/t-km = $510k; dross out $5k; buyback/reverse-logistics $615k
2.5% x $52M baseline property insurance + 25% hurricane rider = $1.65M; G&A + taxes (Act 60-2019 4% corp rate) $0.65M

medium.source: Inherited from prior baseline; cross-checked with BLS OEWS PR 2024, EIA 2024, AON PR market update 2024.

medium.math: 15,000 t x 95% yield x $2,100/t net realized (LME $2,400 - 12.5% discount) = $29.9M, rounded to $30M gross; +dross $270k +tip-fee $900k +carbon-credits $351k +EPR $1,500k +TIP-style incentive $500k (subject to PR legislation; treat as upside) = $33M total. lme.com/metals/non-ferrous/lme-aluminium-ubc-scrap-us-argus

small.irr_assumption: Single-equity, 20-yr horizon, LME price band $1,800-2,400/t realized; LME-sensitive, bottom-of-band at $1,800/t.

medium.irr_assumption: Single-equity, 20-yr horizon, LME price band $1,800-2,400/t realized, IRA Section 45X eligibility holds (10-yr stream at $580/t secondary aluminum, $5.2M Year-1 monetization via Section 6418).

large.irr_assumption: Single-equity, 20-yr horizon, P1020A-grade pricing premium, full 45X capture.

fundingSources.note: 4% corporate income tax (vs 37% federal + 18.5% PR pre-Act 60), 90% property tax exemption, 100% municipal license/GRT exemption 5-10 yr, 50%/100% bonus depreciation. Recycling qualifies under Subtitle B Chapter 6 'Manufacturing Activities'. PV at 8% / 15 yr = ~$2.0-2.5M/yr tax-incentive value, or ~$30M nominal.

40-yr amortizing, investment-grade w/ anchor offtake; modeled on NSW CDS (TOMRA-operated) bond structure. Coverage: 5c deposit x 1.4B cans x 15% unredeemed = $10.5M/yr breakage, supports $130-160M of 40-yr par at 5.5% coupon at 1.4x DSCR. Bond rating target: A/A- (Moody's) at 5.0-5.5% pricing.
fundingSources.confidence: LOW (contingent on PR DRS legislation, PS 369 2024 session not yet passed)

fundingSources.source: Anchor partner CAPEX (Coca-Cola, Ball, Novelis, Crown)
fundingSources.note: Long-term offtake agreements pegged to LME, with 5-10% anchor capital participation. Novelis committed ~$365M to UBC capacity expansion at Utsunomiya (Japan) and Pyeongtaek (Korea) 2022. Closed Loop Partners + American Beverage financed multi-million-dollar San Antonio facility Aug 2024 (60,000 t/yr PET + 20,000 t/yr aluminum).
fundingSources.citation: American Beverage/Closed Loop Partners Aug 2024 americanbeverage.org/press-releases/american-beverage-closed-loop-partners-finance-multi-million-dollar-loan-to-support-new-san-antonio-recycling-facility/; Novelis 2022 via Recycling Today
fundingSources.confidence: MEDIUM (LOI-stage, no signed agreement as of 2026-07-03)

fundingSources.note: 15-20 yr, sub-5% fixed; eligible under EU Global Gateway + IDB Climate Action. PR is an IDB non-borrowing member but continues to be eligible for IDB Invest products post-2017 hurricane-debt restructuring. EIB template: MM S.p.A. EUR 100M Nov 2024 (15-yr, sub-5%) for circular-economy water/waste program. IDB Invest circular-economy facility: $200M revolving credit 2023.
fundingSources.citation: EIB circular-economy eib.org/en/projects/topics/energy-natural-resources/circular-economy/index; EIB MM S.p.A. 2024-454 eib.org/en/press/all/2024-454; IDB Invest circular-economy facility 2023 idbinvest.org

fundingSources.note: FY2024 cohort: $58M total across U.S.; max $4M/award (Waste Dive Dec 2025). PR is eligible as a U.S. territory. Realistic PR application size: $2-3M. SWIFR = Solid Waste Infrastructure for Recycling program under Save Our Seas Act 2.0 (2020).

IRS final regulations published 28 Oct 2024 (Federal Register 2024-24840) confirm secondary production from recycled material is included in 'produced by the taxpayer' definition. Credit: $0.58/kg ($580/t) of qualifying production, 10-yr duration from placed-in-service date. Year-1 monetization via Section 6418 transferability at 90-95% face value. $8.7M/yr x 10 yr = $87M nominal, $61M PV at 8%. Sow vs. P1020A-grade eligibility is a tax-counsel question (substantial transformation requirement at 1.45X-1(c)(2)).
fundingSources.citation: Treasury/IRS final regs federalregister.gov/documents/2024/10/28/2024-24840/advanced-manufacturing-production-credit; Grant Thornton grantthornton.com/insights/alerts/tax/2024/flash/irs-expands-section-45x-advanced-manufacturing-production-credit; Plante Moran plantemoran.com/explore-our-thinking/insight/2024/11/final-45x-guidance-clarifies-advanced-manufacturing-production-credit-costs
fundingSources.confidence: MEDIUM (sow vs. P1020A-grade is a tax-counsel question)

fundingSources.source: Verra / Gold Standard carbon credits (revenue, not CAPEX)
fundingSources.note: Listed for completeness; carbon credits are an operating revenue stream, not a CAPEX source. Aluminum-recycling displacement: ~1.3 tCO2e/t avoided (IAI/Ecoinvent). At medium scenario: 1.3 x 15,000 t x $15-25/tCO2e (Verra Q1 2025) = $293k-$488k/yr, included in revenueAnnual.medium.auxiliary. Methodologies: VCS VM0017 (avoidance of process emissions from aluminum recycling), VM0039; Gold Standard metals-recycling displacement. Lead time to first issuance: 12-18 months.
fundingSources.citation: Verra Registry verra.org; VCS methodology VM0017; International Aluminium Institute LCA data

funding_stack_note: Verra / Gold Standard carbon credits are listed for completeness as a revenue stream (not a CAPEX source); see revenueAnnual.medium.auxiliary. CAPEX sources sum exactly to $53.735M = capex.medium.total.
_comment: Verra line previously double-counted at share=0.05 in the original baseline; that share was inherited from the prior file but caused a 5% over-fund. Now normalized: Verra is $0 of CAPEX (it is revenue) and is captured in revenueAnnual.medium.auxiliary instead.

sanity_check.industry_band_source: Country-cases.md benchmarks; secondary aluminum industry trade-press consensus 2023-2024
sanity_check.flag: Both $/t-invested and $/t-processed are above the industry-expected ranges for all three scenarios. The gap is concentrated in (a) PR industrial electricity ($0.18/kWh vs U.S. $0.0857/kWh = +$215/t OPEX over a U.S. peer) and (b) FORTIFIED Gold hurricane-rated construction (+$700/t CAPEX over a mainland U.S. peer). Gap narrows at scale. Medium is the defensible minimum scale.

solar_storage_add_on.rationale: 1.5 MW solar + 2 MWh battery displaces 30% of grid electricity; +$4M CAPEX ($270/t), -$1.0M/yr OPEX ($67/t). Net positive on $/t-processed.

sanity_check.confidence: HIGH that the gap is real and PR-premium-driven; MEDIUM that a renewable add-on closes the gap (depends on Act 17-2019 net-metering policy and PREPA approval timeline).

data_gaps_remaining: PR-specific itemized equipment quotes (Hertwich, StrikoWestofen) are not in the public domain; line-item CAPEX is based on industry trade-press product literature 2024 and engineering brief §3. Confidence: MEDIUM.

Section 45X sow-casting eligibility under 1.45X-1(c)(2) 'substantial transformation' is a tax-counsel question; current eligibility is interpreted as LIKELY ELIGIBLE based on Grant Thornton / Plante Moran summaries of the Oct 2024 final regs but a formal opinion at financial close is required.
PR DRS legislation (PS 369 2024 session) is not yet enacted; DRS-secured revenue bond funding is conditional. Alternative: EIB/IDB tranche expansion + SWIFR expansion.
Anchor partner LOIs not yet signed; outreach in progress per engineering brief §4. Treat 10-15% anchor contribution as a negotiating target.
LME Aluminium UBC Scrap US (Argus) forward price curve beyond 24 months is not publicly disclosed; the hedge ratio of 60% is a planning assumption.

Carbon-credit spot and offtake pricing for aluminum-recycling displacement is volatile; Verra Q1 2025 range $15-25/tCO2e used. 2024 voluntary market saw 50% price decline; the 2025-2026 trajectory is uncertain.

key_risks.mitigation: Hedge 60% of forward sales 24 mo forward via LME 3-mo + Argus UBC basis.
Pre-design deal stack to allow substitution: EIB/IDB additional tranche + SWIFR expansion.

key_risks.risk: Section 45X sow-casting eligibility denied on audit
key_risks.mitigation: Tax-counsel opinion at financial close; dual casting flexibility (sow or P1020A-grade).
FORTIFIED Gold design; 30-day feedstock stockpile; phased CAPEX allows re-start.

key_risks.risk: Insurance market remains hard (>3% of CAPEX)
key_risks.mitigation: Multi-year binder; captive insurance option for hurricane layer.
LOI before financial close; second-anchor backup (PepsiCo / AB InBev).
Diversify VCS + Gold Standard; bilateral offtake locks price.
Fixed-price EPC contract; 15% contingency in baseline.

companion_files.scenarios_note: Small and large scenarios with full line-by-line CAPEX/OPEX are in the companion file to keep budget-data.json under the 12 KB design-budget hint.

engineering_validation.secondary_aluminum_kwh_per_t: 700-750 (IAI, Discovery Alert 2026) — 95% energy reduction vs. primary
engineering_validation.lme_ubc_futures: AAAC contract (LME, listed 2024) — used for 24-mo forward hedge
safety_flags: Molten aluminum + water = violent explosion (NIOSH 1975, CAMEO Chemicals). Salt-flux + dross handling per OSHA 1910.119 + NFPA 484 mandatory.
Permitting pathway: DRNA minor NSR (PR lead) + EPA Region 2 PSD (only if >250 tpy CO/PM10 threshold; medium ~140 tpy stays minor).
Operator minimum staffing: at least 1 trained 'Hertwich-certified' furnace operator per shift (vendor training cert).
data_gaps_listed_by_engineers: PREB industrial ¢/kWh by tariff class for 1.5 MW load
PR industrial natural gas delivered price at 14 MMBtu/h interruptible
FORTIFIED Commercial PR insurance premium data
PR salary benchmarks for process operators
JCA minor NSR actual cycle time for a UBC plant
